summary:
Smallpox has been officially eradicated worldwide since 1980; therefore, it is exceedingly rare or virtually non-existent in pediatric populations due to global immunizations and lack of naturally occurring cases over decades now passed from last known outbreak. Summary:

Smallpox, having been eradicated globally since 1980, is exceptionally rare if not nonexistent in children today owing to widespread vaccinations and absence of new outbreaks.
